mlr3mbspls 0.3.3
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Quick Start with mlr3mbspls
  • Changelog

Block-wise quality-control summary for TaskMultiBlock objects

Source: R/mb_task_overview.R
mb_task_overview.Rd

mb_task_overview() creates a compact, analysis-ready overview of a multi-block task. It is designed as an early quality-control step before fitting MB-sPLS, MB-sPLS-XY, or MB-sPCA pipelines.

The returned object contains:

  • overview: task-level dimensions and complete-case rates,

  • blocks: one row per block with feature counts, type counts, missingness, and constant-feature counts,

  • target: a one-row summary for supervised tasks,

  • target_distribution: class counts for classification tasks,

  • issues: a compact table of actionable warnings/notes.

This helper is particularly useful in clinical, social-science, and multi-omics workflows where block imbalance, missingness, and low-information variables should be checked explicitly before resampling or interpretation.

For TaskMultiBlock() objects, task$overview() is the primary interface. mb_task_overview(task) is retained as a convenience wrapper for ad hoc code and backward compatibility.

mb_task_overview(
  task,
  rows = NULL,
  blocks = NULL,
  include_target = TRUE,
  top_levels = 5L
)

Arguments

task

A task created with TaskMultiBlock() or any mlr3::Task that carries multiblock metadata in task$blocks or task$extra_args$blocks.

rows

Optional row ids to summarize. Defaults to all task rows.

blocks

Optional block subset. Either a character vector of block names or a named block mapping.

include_target

Logical; summarize the target if the task is supervised.

top_levels

Maximum number of classification levels to print in the target summary. All levels are still returned in target_distribution.

Value

A named list with data.table components: overview, blocks, target, target_distribution, and issues.

Details

Summarize a multi-block task

Examples

task = mlr3::tsk("mbspls_synthetic_classif")
qc = task$overview()
qc$overview
#>                     task_id task_type n_rows n_blocks n_features
#>                      <char>    <char>  <int>    <int>      <int>
#> 1: mbspls_synthetic_classif   classif     90        3         18
#>    n_complete_rows complete_row_rate  target
#>              <int>             <num>  <char>
#> 1:              90                 1 subtype
qc$blocks
#>      block n_features n_numeric n_factor n_character n_logical
#>     <char>      <int>     <int>    <int>       <int>     <int>
#> 1: block_a          6         6        0           0         0
#> 2: block_b          6         6        0           0         0
#> 3: block_c          6         6        0           0         0
#>    n_constant_numeric n_missing_cells pct_missing_cells n_complete_rows
#>                 <int>           <int>             <num>           <int>
#> 1:                  0               0                 0              90
#> 2:                  0               0                 0              90
#> 3:                  0               0                 0              90
#>    complete_row_rate
#>                <num>
#> 1:                 1
#> 2:                 1
#> 3:                 1
qc$issues
#> Empty data.table (0 rows and 5 cols): scope,item,severity,issue,message

Contents
